scenario.title: First Trial — Greeting Service
scenario.narrative: You are reviewing a tiny greeting helper. Every line of the function must execute at least once for Statement coverage.

claim: Did the test set below execute every statement of greet() at least once?
charges: The defendant function greet(name) is presented for Statement coverage review.
The prosecution alleges that a single test exercising one greeting input is sufficient to claim 100% Statement coverage of greet().

options.label: Yes — both lines (the assignment and the return) run when T1 fires.
options.explanation: Correct. T1 calls greet('World'), which executes the assignment AND the return. With no decisions in this function, Statement coverage is the strongest applicable level.

options.label: No — the return statement is never reached by T1.
options.explanation: Incorrect. There are no early-exit branches in greet(); once the assignment runs, control falls straight into the return.

correct_answer_explanation: Statement coverage = 100%. T1 hits both executable lines.
wrong_answer_explanation: Statement coverage only asks whether each line ran at least once. Trace T1 line by line — both run.
hints: Statement coverage only asks: did every line of code execute at least once?
There are 2 executable lines (the assignment and the return). Did T1 hit both?
T1 runs both lines exactly once, so Statement coverage is 100%. Note: there are no decisions to test here, so Statement is the strongest applicable level.
